method: Outside-in crawl (browser UA, robots-respecting — nyc.gov/robots.txt only disallows /html/misc/). Fingerprinted the OTI informational site nyc.gov/content/oti (Akamai + nginx + NYC.gov 'Livesite' platform v22 + mPulse + Dynatrace) and probed the citywide platforms OTI OPERATES: the api.nyc.gov gateway (identified as Microsoft Azure API Management from Request-Context appId + 401 'missing subscription key'; GeoClient/GeoSearch are key-gated), the GeoSearch/Pelias geocoder at geosearch.planninglabs.nyc (200), and the NYC Open Data platform on Socrata/Tyler (X-Socrata-Region/RequestId). Verified the NYC Open Data agency label 'Office of Technology and Innovation (OTI)' via the Socrata Discovery API (221 assets) and pulled all 221 with column schemas.
headline_findings: OTI is the META case: it is not an agency with data trapped in HTML — it OPERATES the city's data infrastructure. It runs NYC Open Data (data.cityofnewyork.us on Socrata/Tyler), the api.nyc.gov gateway (Microsoft Azure API Management, hosting GeoClient and GeoSearch), NYC.gov itself, LinkNYC, the municipal broadband program, public Wi-Fi, and the 311 pipeline.

As a publisher it is enormous: 221 datasets under the 'Office of Technology and Innovation (OTI)' label — including the most-viewed dataset in the entire city (311 Service Requests, erm2-nwe9, 1.26M+ views), plus the catalog OF ITSELF (the LL251 Published Data Asset Inventory 5tqd-u88y and the Open Data Release Tracker qj2z-ibhs), LinkNYC, broadband, Wi-Fi, planimetric base-map, and .nyc domain data.

The api.nyc.gov gateway is real and running (Azure APIM) but has no owned, machine-readable catalog of its services and no self-service key API — GeoClient returns HTTP 401 'missing subscription key' with keys provisioned only through a developer portal.

The reframe is unique: because the data is already open and the gateway already exists, the modernization is not to LIBERATE data but to PRODUCTIZE the operator role — a single owned gateway + catalog API that lets a developer or agent discover a dataset, discover a gateway service, geocode an address, and register an asset through one contract. OTI is the natural home for the project's nyc-commons + registry ideas.

Net-new write surfaces: register a dataset to the catalog, and request an api.nyc.gov gateway key — the two things a platform operator should offer programmatically but today does only through internal or portal workflows. See tech-stack.md and apis-observed.md.

apis_observed.owner: NYC OTI (on Microsoft Azure API Management)
apis_observed.note: The citywide API gateway OTI operates. Request-Context appId + 'missing subscription key' fingerprint Azure APIM. Hosts GeoClient (address/BBL/BIN geocoding) and other agency APIs. No owned catalog of services, no self-service key API.

apis_observed.owner: NYC (DCP Planning Labs; wraps OTI GeoSupport)
apis_observed.note: Open Pelias/Mapzen geocoder over NYC GeoSupport — returns coordinates + geography spine. The open twin of GeoClient. HTTP 200, no key.

The open-data platform OTI operates for the WHOLE city. SODA /resource/<id>.json per dataset + the Socrata Discovery/catalog API. OTI itself publishes 221 datasets, incl. the catalog of the entire corpus.
Content only — About OTI, services, initiatives. Akamai edge, nginx, mPulse + Dynatrace RUM. No content API.
NYC.gov ID / citywide identity — itself an OTI-run platform.
server-timing ak_p / mPulse on the OTI site.

fruit.name: NYC Open Data catalog (the catalog itself)
opendata_match: Local Law 251 Published Data Asset Inventory 5tqd-u88y (26c)
NYC Open Data Release Tracker qj2z-ibhs (12c)
Open Data Help Desk: Public Dataset Requests 63us-eqtq
fruit.notes: The META entity: OTI operates the platform and publishes the catalog of itself. An owned catalog API would list/get/register any of the 2,300+ assets, keyed on the Socrata four-by-four UID.

fruit.name: api.nyc.gov gateway services (GeoClient / GeoSearch)
fruit.notes: OTI RUNS the citywide gateway but exposes no machine-readable catalog of its services and no self-service key API. GeoClient returns 401 'missing subscription key'. The net-new surfaces: a services catalog + a request_api_key write.

fruit.name: Geocoding (GeoClient / GeoSearch)
fruit.notes: Address/intersection -> coordinates + geography spine (BBL/BIN/borough/council district). GeoSearch (Pelias) is open; GeoClient on api.nyc.gov needs a key. The one universal operation every other NYC domain depends on.

fruit.name: LinkNYC kiosks
opendata_match: LinkNYC New Site Permit Applications xp25-gxux
fruit.notes: The franchise public Wi-Fi/comms kiosks OTI oversees. Locations + live Wi-Fi/tablet/phone status + usage. Keyed on Site ID.

fruit.name: Municipal broadband assets
opendata_match: Universal Solicitation for Broadband Asset Dataset 2bsr-c6qq (31c)
Universal Solicitation of Broadband Target Neighborhoods 2qqs-crk2 (7c)
Internet Master Plan: Adoption & Infrastructure by Neighborhood fg5j-q5nk (77c)
fruit.notes: Fiber/wireless readiness of buildings in broadband target neighborhoods, from OTI's municipal-broadband / Internet Master Plan program. Keyed on id + BBL/BIN.

fruit.name: Public Wi-Fi hotspots
fruit.notes: The citywide inventory of free/partner/limited public Wi-Fi — libraries, parks, LinkNYC, transit, provider networks. Keyed on OBJECTID / DOITT_ID + SSID.

fruit.name: 311 Service Requests
opendata_match: 311 Service Requests from 2020 to Present erm2-nwe9 (48c)
fruit.notes: OTI operates the 311 open-data pipeline that routes each request to the responsible agency. The city's flagship dataset; keyed on Unique Key.

fruit.name: Register a dataset to the catalog
fruit.notes: NET-NEW write: today publishing an asset is an internal Socrata + Open Data Team workflow with no public API. An owned catalog API would let agencies (and agents acting for them) register an asset for the LL251 inventory.

fruit.name: Request an api.nyc.gov gateway key
fruit.notes: NET-NEW write: gateway keys are provisioned through a developer portal, not an API — api.nyc.gov returns 401 until one is granted. A self-service request_api_key operation would productize the operator role.

fruit.name: Planimetric base map & building footprints
fruit.notes: OTI's GIS division publishes the citywide base map — buildings, sidewalks, roadbed, elevation, land cover. Dozens of the 221 assets. The geospatial foundation other agencies build on.

fruit.name: NYC Websites Inventory 2023 (Digital Accessibility Report Appendix 3)
fruit.notes: Citywide inventory of NYC agency websites assessed for the 2023 Digital Accessibility Report (LL26); a per-site registry ideal as an API.

fruit.name: WCAG 2.1 Survey Tool (Digital Accessibility Report Appendix 1)
fruit.notes: Structured WCAG 2.1 success-criteria survey/checklist used to audit NYC sites.

fruit.name: NYC Digital Accessibility Report 2025 - Appendix 1
fruit.notes: Data appendix to the 2025 NYC Digital Accessibility Report.

fruit.name: NYC Digital Accessibility Report 2025 - Appendix 3
fruit.notes: Data appendix to the 2025 NYC Digital Accessibility Report.

fruit.name: NYC Digital Accessibility Report 2021 - Appendix 1
fruit.notes: 2021 report site inventory / audit results (largest appendix).

meta.platform: NYC.gov 'Livesite' informational site (Akamai + nginx + mPulse + Dynatrace); OTI OPERATES NYC Open Data (Socrata/Tyler), the api.nyc.gov gateway (Microsoft Azure API Management; GeoClient/GeoSearch), and the 311 pipeline.
meta.netnew: One owned, unified gateway + catalog API — plus two write surfaces: register a dataset, and request an api.nyc.gov gateway key.
meta.tagline: The agency that runs the city's data infrastructure, made into a first-class API product.
meta.headline: OTI already operates NYC Open Data, the api.nyc.gov gateway, and 311 — the modernization is to productize that operator role into one owned, agent-native citywide gateway + catalog API.

scorecard_notes.platform: Operates the city's platforms (Socrata, Azure APIM, Livesite) — it IS the platform layer.
scorecard_notes.siteApi: The OTI site is HTML with no content API, but OTI runs a real API gateway (api.nyc.gov).
scorecard_notes.openData: 221 datasets incl. the catalog of itself and the city's most-viewed dataset (311).
scorecard_notes.coreData: Core entities all open — LinkNYC, broadband, Wi-Fi, 311, planimetric base map.
scorecard_notes.writeApi: No public write API; dataset publishing and gateway keys are internal/portal-only.
scorecard_notes.agent: No agent-native surface today; MCP proposed here.
scorecard_notes.ownership: Operates the stack but on vendor platforms (Socrata/Tyler + Microsoft Azure APIM).
